name: Cabezo de Piskerra Loop Trail
name_original: Parque Natural de las Bardenas Reales – circular hasta el Piskerra y el Cabezo de Piskerra
description: Jog a moderate 3.1-mile loop through the lunar-like landscape of Bardenas Reales, with panoramic views from Piskerra and Cabezo de Piskerra.
region_intro: Jogging in Bardenas Reales offers a unique experience through a semi-desert landscape in southeast Navarra, Spain. The region is characterized by dramatic erosion-sculpted formations, including canyons, plateaus, and isolated hills known as "cabezos." Its terrain features both expansive plains in Bardena Blanca and higher plateaus with pine forests in Bardena Negra, providing varied running conditions. An extensive network of paths and trails spans over 700 kilometers, suitable for different running preferences. Best jogging routes in Bardenas Reales The most popular jogging route is Cabezo de Piskerra Loop Trail, a 3.1 miles (5.0 km) trail that takes about 40 minutes to complete. This route explores the area around the notable Piskerra rock formation. Another top favourite among local runners is Bardenas Reales Natural Park – Cortinas Reservoir loop from Cabezo de la Muga, a moderate 7.9 miles (12.7 km) path. This route offers views of the Cortinas Reservoir within the natural park. Local runners also love the View from Peña del Fraile loop from Corral de Bea, a 2.8 miles (4.5 km) trail leading through the diverse landscape, often completed in about 40 minutes. Jogging in Bardenas Reales is defined by its semi-desert landscape, erosion-sculpted formations, and contrasting areas like Bardena Blanca and Bardena Negra. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy paths to more challenging trails. The routes in Bardenas Reales are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.1 stars from 6 reviews. More than 700 runners have used komoot to explore Bardenas Reales's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Bardenas Reales?
A: Bardenas Reales offers a wide selection of over 100 running routes. These trails traverse the unique semi-desert landscape, providing diverse options for joggers of all levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when running in Bardenas Reales?
A: The terrain in Bardenas Reales is incredibly varied, shaped by millions of years of erosion. You'll encounter a 'lunar landscape' with dramatic clay, chalk, and sandstone formations, including canyons, plateaus, and isolated hills. The central Bardena Blanca features expansive plains, while the southeastern Bardena Negra offers higher plateaus with pine forests. Routes include flat stretches, gently rising land, and paths winding around gullies.

Q: What is the best time of year to go jogging in Bardenas Reales?
A: Spring is considered the ideal time for jogging in Bardenas Reales, as temperatures are pleasant and the landscape reveals a greener side. It's advisable to avoid extremely hot summers and periods of heavy rainfall, as the heat can be intense and rain can make trails muddy and difficult to traverse.

Q: Are there any easy running trails suitable for beginners or casual joggers?
A: Yes, Bardenas Reales has options for all abilities. There are 11 easy running routes available, perfect for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed jog through the stunning scenery.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Bardenas Reales?
A: The running routes in Bardenas Reales are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.1 stars from 6 reviews. Runners often praise the unique, dramatic landscape and the varied terrain that makes each run an adventure. Over 700 runners have used komoot to explore this region.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Bardenas Reales?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Bardenas Reales are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e971502808/cabezo-de-piskerra-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabezo de Piskerra Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular option exploring the area around the notable Piskerra rock formation.

Q: What landmarks or natural attractions can I see along the running routes?
A: Bardenas Reales is rich in natural wonders. You can encounter iconic formations like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/945742" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castildetierra&lt;/a&gt;, a striking clay pyramid, or the prominent &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3845864" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña del Fraile&lt;/a&gt;. Many routes offer panoramic views from spots like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3927882" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bardena Blanca Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, showcasing the park's unique erosion-sculpted landscape. You might also pass through areas like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5779323" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranco Grande&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any running routes that pass by the Cortinas Reservoir?
A: Yes, you can find running routes that offer views of the Cortinas Reservoir. A notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10464721" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bardenas Reales Natural Park – Cortinas Reservoir loop from Cabezo de la Muga&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 7.9-mile path that provides scenic views of the reservoir within the natural park.

Q: Is it possible to see wildlife while jogging in Bardenas Reales?
A: Despite its arid appearance, Bardenas Reales supports diverse flora and fauna. It's home to over 60 species of birds, making it a point of interest for birdwatchers. While jogging, you might spot various birds and other wildlife adapted to this unique semi-desert environment.

Q: Are there any specific rules or permits required for running in Bardenas Reales?
A: Visitors are reminded to stay on regulated paths to protect the natural environment. Climbing mountain passes and venturing off-trail is forbidden. There are no specific permits required for running on the designated trails, but always respect the park's regulations.

Q: Can I find running routes that offer views of Piskerra?
A: Absolutely. The Piskerra rock formation is a prominent feature in Bardenas Reales. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17544776" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bardena Blanca Viewpoint – Piskerra - Cabezo de Piskerra loop from Cabezo de Piskerra&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent moderate route that specifically includes Piskerra, offering great views of this geological wonder.
tips:
The landscape from up here is surreal, it looks like something out of a science fiction movie. In itself, touring the Bardenas Reales is very interesting, but if you add reaching the top of Piskerra, the views will leave you speechless.
From this crossroad you will have the Piskerra on one side and the Cabezo on the other, it is worth visiting both, very beautiful views of all of the Bardenas.
Be careful on the way up, there will be sectors with some loose terrain but with a firm step you will arrive without problems.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to access the Parque Natural de las Bardenas Reales?
faqs.answer: Generally, there is no entrance fee or specific permit required for general access to the Parque Natural de las Bardenas Reales. However, as a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve, certain areas or routes, especially during specific seasons, might have restrictions or temporary closures to protect nesting birds. It's always a good idea to check the official park website for the latest regulations before your visit, particularly between February and September.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this circular route?
faqs.answer: Parking is typically available near the trailhead for the Piskerra and Cabezo de Piskerra circular route within the Bardenas Reales. Look for designated parking areas as you approach the starting point of the trail. Always ensure you park in marked or permitted areas to avoid issues.

faqs.question: Is this circular route suitable for beginners or those new to jogging?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is generally considered to be of easy to moderate difficulty, making it suitable for a range of fitness levels, including beginners. While some sections are unpaved, the paths are described as easily accessible. The climb up to Piskerra is also considered easy, offering great views without excessive challenge.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Piskerra and Cabezo de Piskerra trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied and characteristic of a semi-desert landscape. You'll encounter unpaved sections, natural paths, and some gravel. The ground is primarily composed of clay, gypsum, and sandstone, which can be susceptible to erosion, creating diverse formations. While generally manageable, joggers should be mindful of the natural surface.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: This trail offers stunning 'lunar-like' scenery with impressive badlands, deep ravines, and eroded plateaus. The highlight of the route is reaching Piskerra - Cabezo de Piskerra, which provides magnificent 360-degree panoramic views of the unique desert-like environment. You'll witness the otherworldly geological formations that make the Bardenas Reales famous.

faqs.question: Is the Parque Natural de las Bardenas Reales – circular to Piskerra and Cabezo de Piskerra trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the Bardenas Reales generally allows dogs, it's crucial to keep them on a leash to protect the delicate ecosystem and wildlife, especially during nesting seasons. Always ensure your dog is well-behaved and that you clean up after them to preserve the natural beauty of the park.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to jog this route is typically during the cooler months of spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). Summers can be extremely hot, making jogging challenging, while winters can be cold. Spring and autumn offer more pleasant temperatures and are ideal for enjoying the unique landscape.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route does intersect with other paths. Approximately 25% of the circular route follows sections of the official trail known as "Vuelta al Polígono de tiro." This means you might encounter other hikers or joggers on these shared segments.

faqs.question: Are there any specific safety considerations for jogging in Bardenas Reales?
faqs.answer: Given the semi-desert environment, it's essential to carry sufficient water, especially during warmer periods, as there are no water sources on the trail. Sun protection (hat, sunscreen) is also highly recommended due to limited shade. Always inform someone of your plans and be aware of the weather conditions, which can change rapidly.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: The Bardenas Reales is an excellent spot for bird watching, and you might encounter various species adapted to this arid environment. While larger mammals are less common, you may spot reptiles or smaller desert animals. Remember to observe wildlife from a distance and avoid disturbing their natural habitat.
